system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

simulationType  RAS;

RAS

    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;

thermoType

mixture

    }

boundaryField
{
    Default_Boundary_Region
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    inlet
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='epsilon', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content="FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   0.001;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet straight section (along x-axis)
    // Face at x = -50: y = ±25, z = ±25
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 0
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3

    // Face at x = 0: y = ±25, z = ±25
    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    // Corner point
    (  25   0  25 )   // 8
    (  25   0 -25 )   // 9

    // Inner corner of bend (where x=y plane meets)
    (  25  -50  25 )  // 10
    (  25  -50 -25 )  // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight section (extending in -y direction)
    (  25  -75  25 )  // 12
    (  25  -75 -25 )  // 13
    (  25 -125 -25 )  // 14
    (  25 -125  25 )  // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    // Bottom face (z=-25): 0->1->2->3, Top face (z=25): 4->5->6->7
    // But we need to ensure proper right-hand rule
    // Let's define bottom face CCW when viewed from +z: 0(-50,25,-25), 3(-50,-25,-25), 2(-50,-25,25), 1(-50,25,25)
    // Actually, let's make it simpler: face at x=-50: 0,1,2,3 (CCW from +z)
    // face at x=0: 4,5,6,7 (CCW from +z)
    // hex (bottom_face) (top_face)
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    // We need to go from x=0 plane to y=-50 plane
    // Bottom face at y=-50: 11(25,-50,-25), 10(25,-50,25), 8(25,0,25), 9(25,0,-25)
    // Top face at y=0: 9(25,0,-25), 8(25,0,25), 10(25,-50,25), 11(25,-50,-25)
    // Wait, let me redefine this more carefully
    // Bottom face (y=-50): 11(25,-50,-25), 10(25,-50,25), 8(25,0,25), 9(25,0,-25)
    // Top face (y=0): 9(25,0,-25), 8(25,0,25), 10(25,-50,25), 11(25,-50,-25)
    // Need to check orientation... let's use: 9,8,10,11 for top face
    hex (9 8 10 11 8 10 11 9) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section
    // Extending in -y direction from y=-75 to y=-125
    // At y=-75: 13(25,-75,-25), 12(25,-75,25), 15(25,-125,25), 14(25,-125,-25)
    // At y=-125: 14(25,-125,-25), 15(25,-125,25), 13(25,-75,25), 12(25,-75,-25)
    hex (13 12 15 14 12 15 14 13) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (12 13 14 15)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 1 5 4)
            (3 7 6 2)
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 2 6 5)

            // Block 1 walls
            (8 10 11 9)
            (9 11 10 8)

            // Block 2 walls
            (12 15 14 13)
            (13 14 15 12)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //"), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1500;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform 300;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            flowRateInletVelocity;
        massFlowRate    0.5;
        profile         turbulentBL;
        value           uniform (0 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            pressureInletOutletVelocity;
        value           uniform (0 0 0);
    }

    walls
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

boundaryField
{

        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 110000;

        type            zeroGradient;

}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.rhoSimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_20260516_195157_run3/runs/squareBend/2/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 52.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) is "inside-out"

The block definition `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` produces an inside-out block because the vertex ordering doesn't follow the right-hand rule. The fundamental problem is that the **vertex positions themselves** are arranged such that connecting them in the order `(0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` creates a degenerate/inverted hexahedron.

Let me analyze the vertex coordinates:
- Vertex 0: (-50, 25, 25)
- Vertex 1: (-50, 25, -25)
- Vertex 2: (-50, -25, -25)
- Vertex 3: (-50, -25, 25)
- Vertex 4: (0, 25, 25)
- Vertex 5: (0, 25, -25)
- Vertex 6: (0, -25, -25)
- Vertex 7: (0, -25, 25)

If we visualize this: vertices 0-3 are at x=-50 forming a rectangle in the y-z plane, and vertices 4-7 are at x=0 forming another identical rectangle. When we connect 0→1→2→3 as the "bottom" face and 4→5→6→7 as the "top" face, the issue is that the winding directions aren't consistent. Specifically, looking at face 0-1-2-3 from outside (+x direction), the winding 0→1→2→3 goes clockwise instead of counterclockwise.

**The fix:** Swap the vertex ordering so that both faces wind consistently. Try:

```
hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)
```

Here, face 0-3-2-1 winds CCW when viewed from +x, and face 4-7-6-5 also winds CCW when viewed from +x. Alternatively, the standard approach for two parallel rectangular faces is often:

```
hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)  --> WRONG (as currently failing)
hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)  --> CORRECTED winding
```

However, given that the previous attempt already tried `hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` and got "inside-out", the real issue might be deeper — the **entire vertex layout** needs to be reconsidered.

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cascading failure (no mesh exists)

Since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh was generated, so `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot proceed. This resolves itself once `blockMesh` works.

## Recommended Approach — Redesign the Mesh Geometry

The current vertex layout places all vertices at x=-50 and x=0 for the first block, but the bend transitions in the y-direction starting at vertex 8 at (25, 0, 25). This creates geometrically incompatible blocks. Here’s a better approach:

### Option: Single Straight Duct (Simplified but Valid)

Given the complexity, simplify to a **single straight duct** running in the x-direction from x=-50 to some positive x, eliminating the complex multi-block bend entirely. Remove vertices 8-15 and the bend-related blocks. Keep only:

- Vertices 0-3 at x=-50 (y=±25, z=±25)
- Vertices 4-7 at x=+L (say x=+50, y=±25, z=±25)

Then the block would be:
```
hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

With boundary patches:
- `inlet` at x=-50: faces (0 3 2 1)
- `outlet` at x=+50: faces (4 7 6 5)
- `walls`: the remaining 4 side faces

This guarantees a valid mesh. The user specified a "squareBend" tutorial — if the bend is essential, study the actual squareBend tutorial `blockMeshDict` carefully and replicate its proven vertex ordering and block topology exactly.

### Key Fix Checklist

1. **Fix Block 0 vertex ordering** to `hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)` or equivalent correct winding.
2. **Verify all 8 vertices of each hex block** actually form a convex parallelepiped — no coplanar or concave configurations.
3. **Ensure all boundary patch names** in `0/T`, `0/U`, `0/p` match those in `blockMeshDict` (use `walls` instead of individual named wall patches unless absolutely necessary).
4. **Re-run `blockMesh`** after corrections, then proceed to `rhoSimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change Block 0 hex definition from hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) to hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) to fix inside-out block by correcting vertex winding; Change Block 1 hex definition from hex (9 8 10 11 8 10 11 9) to hex (8 9 11 10) which defines a proper tetrahedral-like wedge for the bend corner; Replace Block 2 hex definition from hex (13 12 15 14 12 15 14 13) to hex (12 13 14 15) which is a valid single hex for the outlet section; Update Block 0 internal face count from (10 10 10) to (10 10 10) keeping same; Update Block 1 internal face count from (10 10 10) to (10 10 10) keeping same; Update Block 2 internal face count from (100 10 10) to (10 10 10) since geometry changed; Rewrite entire boundary section to have only three patches: inlet with faces (0 3 2 1), outlet with faces (12 13 14 15), walls with all remaining external faces from all three blocks listed individually'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
